plan.goal: Close every remaining implementation gap in docs/PRISM_SHARED_COORDINATION_REFS.md by demoting branch-local mirrors, separating branch publication from coordination publication, completing all integration modes and evidence models, tightening lease and heartbeat semantics, hardening degraded and recovery paths, and validating the full shared-ref model against the design doc section by section.
plan.title: Finish PRISM shared coordination refs end to end

graph.title: Finish PRISM shared coordination refs end to end
graph.goal: Close every remaining implementation gap in docs/PRISM_SHARED_COORDINATION_REFS.md by demoting branch-local mirrors, separating branch publication from coordination publication, completing all integration modes and evidence models, tightening lease and heartbeat semantics, hardening degraded and recovery paths, and validating the full shared-ref model against the design doc section by section.

nodes.title: Freeze the final section-by-section shared-ref gap matrix and acceptance contract
nodes.summary: Turn the current doc audit into an explicit tracked contract so every remaining partial section in PRISM_SHARED_COORDINATION_REFS.md has an owner, boundary, and definition of done before more code lands.

acceptance.label: Every partial or not-implemented section in PRISM_SHARED_COORDINATION_REFS.md is mapped to concrete owning code paths and an implementation task.
The repo records which shared-ref design points are done, partial, or intentionally deferred with no ambiguous remaining gaps.
Follow-on tasks can use this matrix as the authoritative checklist for closing the doc end to end.

nodes.title: Demote branch-local shared coordination mirrors to minimal derived exports
nodes.summary: Stop mirroring full shared coordination authority into branch-local `.prism/state/**` by default and leave only bounded, explicitly derived exports so the shared ref becomes the real source of truth.

acceptance.label: Branch-local `.prism/state/**` no longer persists full shared coordination mirrors by default; remaining files are explicitly marked derived and non-authoritative.
Query, hydration, and MCP views remain correct when branch-local mirrors are absent, stale, or intentionally pruned.
Tests prove shared-ref reads and operator workflows still work after removing mirror-first assumptions.

nodes.title: Represent branch publication, coordination publication, and target integration as distinct durable states
nodes.summary: Separate branch publication, shared coordination publication, and verified target integration in the task state machine so require-mode workflows can recover cleanly and query surfaces stop collapsing materially different states.

acceptance.label: Task and plan views distinguish `published_to_branch`, `coordination_published`, and `integrated_to_target` without ambiguity.
Require-mode completion only becomes authoritative after shared coordination publication succeeds, with partial publication states preserved durably.
Recovery logic and operator views can resume from any intermediate publication state without manual state surgery.

nodes.title: Add trusted landing records for squash and rebase integration evidence
nodes.summary: Make target-integration verification work for non-merge landings by recording trusted landing evidence that binds rebased or squashed target commits back to the originating task and review artifacts.

acceptance.label: Squash and rebase landings can be verified through trusted PRISM records rather than raw branch reachability alone.
Landing evidence can bind review artifacts, target commits, and task ids without relying on ambiguous commit ancestry.
Ambiguous or unverifiable target commits remain rejected until explicit trusted landing evidence exists.

nodes.title: Complete manual_pr integration mode with review-backed observation and landing verification
nodes.summary: Finish the manual PR mode so PRISM requires review artifacts, observes external PR landings correctly, and upgrades integration state only when landing evidence is trusted and complete.

acceptance.label: `manual_pr` mode blocks integration advancement until a valid review artifact is linked to the task.
PRISM can observe external PR landing and record verified integration for merge, squash, and rebase outcomes.
Tests cover retry, partial failure, and late-observation flows without manual state repair.

nodes.title: Complete auto_pr mode with review artifact creation, update flow, and post-land verification
nodes.summary: Implement the autonomous PR mode end to end so PRISM can create or refresh review artifacts, drive merge enablement within policy, and verify the eventual landing on the target branch.

acceptance.label: `auto_pr` mode can create or update the review artifact and persist the linkage on the task durably.
Policy-approved merge automation can be enabled and its result observed without losing integration evidence.
Failures leave an actionable retry state instead of collapsing to a generic pending status.

nodes.title: Complete direct_integrate mode with trusted direct landing execution and immediate verification
nodes.summary: Finish the direct integration mode so PRISM can land eligible task branches itself under policy, emit trusted landing metadata, and verify the target branch update immediately.

acceptance.label: `direct_integrate` mode checks freshness, validation, branch policy, and target movement preconditions before landing.
Allowed direct landings emit trusted landing metadata that can be verified without external manual repair.
Immediate post-land verification and failure reporting are explicit and tested.

nodes.title: Make scheduling, dependency gating, and query views integration-aware
nodes.summary: Teach scheduling and blocker resolution to distinguish completion, coordination publication, and target integration so downstream work can depend on the right state instead of plain task completion.

acceptance.label: Blockers and dependency checks can gate separately on completion, coordination publication, and verified target integration.
Query surfaces expose the distinct integration-aware lifecycle states without ad hoc interpretation in clients.
Follow-on tasks can explicitly depend on `integrated_to_target` when the target branch state matters.

nodes.title: Finish durable lease publication and low-frequency authoritative renewal on the shared ref
nodes.summary: Complete the lease model so authoritative lease facts, renewals, and staleness decisions live on the shared ref instead of drifting between local heartbeats and branch mirrors.

acceptance.label: Shared coordination refs publish durable lease lifecycle facts and authoritative renewal timestamps.
Renewal writes extend meaningful lease state rather than producing heartbeat-style churn on the shared ref.
Claim recovery and stale-owner detection read authoritative lease facts first and behave correctly after restarts.

nodes.title: Keep assisted heartbeat renewal local, off by default, bounded, and non-authoritative
nodes.summary: Constrain assisted renewal so it helps local liveness only when explicitly enabled and never becomes a substitute for authoritative lease extension on the shared ref.

acceptance.label: Assisted renewal remains off by default and is never treated as authoritative identity or claim proof.
Only authoritative lease extensions publish to the shared ref; local heartbeat assistance stays local and bounded.
Diagnostics clearly distinguish local liveness assistance from durable shared-ref lease state.

nodes.title: Finish compaction, archive-boundary trust continuity, and operator diagnostics
nodes.summary: Complete the operational side of shared coordination refs so compaction preserves trust semantics, archive boundaries stay intelligible, and operators can see enough history and health to debug live systems.

acceptance.label: Compaction preserves explicit trust continuity or archive-boundary semantics instead of discarding verification history implicitly.
Operator surfaces expose current head, last verified manifest, last successful publish, retry counts, and compaction health.
Bounded recent-history and archive/export behavior are explicit, documented, and covered by tests.
